title: pilosella leucopsilon subsp. leucopsilon
intro: Pilosella leucopsilon subsp. leucopsilon, also known as lesser knapweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic and to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, contributing to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Pilosella leucopsilon subsp. leucopsilon can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote overall well-being.
